channel.title: Morning Mail
channel.description: Stay updated on the latest news and events happening in the RPI community.

item.title: Success Center Hosting Review Sessions Week of 4/5-4/11
item.description: Week of 4/5- 4/11: Need some last minute review for your upcoming exam? Stop by for a Puck Cookie and a Review Session with our Success Center Tutors! -Strengths: Monday, 4/6 (5-7pm) at the Success Center (Beta Classroom) -MAU: Monday, 4/6 (5-7pm) at the Success Center
item.author: Students

item.title: Success Lab: Mindfulness
item.description: Date: Tuesday, April 7, 2026 4:00 PM - 5:00 PM - Come learn what all the buzz is about and why companies like Google, Twitter, PayPal, LinkedIn and many more in Silicon Valley are employing mindfulness techniques. We’ll discuss the benefits, particularly in assisting with stress, overall cognition, and how it can help you progress towards your goals.
item.author: Students

item.title: Success Lab: Academic Prep for Finals
item.description: Date: Tuesday, April 7, 2026 4:00 PM - 5:00 PM - Learn how to finish the semester strong through preparation and savvy techniques for your final exams.
item.author: Students

item.title: Returning Students Housing Application Opening
item.description: Date: Wednesday, April 8, 2026 (all day) - The housing application for current students returning for the 2026-2027 school year will open on April 8, 2026.
item.author: Students

item.title: The RPI Experience: What’s New, What’s Next Virtual Session
item.description: Date: Tuesday, April 7, 2026 12:00 PM - 1:00 PM - Curious what’s changing with The RPI Experience (Formerly The Arch) and what it means for you? Come join us for a second live virtual chat where we’ll break down the latest updates, walk through expectations, and help you plan your next steps with confidence. We will also cover key details about Discovery semester registration for summer, fall, and spring, and answer your questions so you feel prepared for what’s ahead!
item.author: Students

item.title: Dining and Flex Dollars Reminder
item.description: Friendly reminder to all students with active residential meal plans that the last day to purchase additional flex dollars for the Spring 2026 semester will be April 27th. The last day to utilize Flex Dollars for Spring 2026 meal plans is May 16th — please keep in mind that flex dollar balances do not carry over at the end of the academic year. Dining Halls close for the Spring 2026 semester on May 8th after dinner. For more information click the link above.
item.author: Students

item.title: Faculty Spotlight - Johnson Samuel
item.description: Date: Wednesday, April 8, 2026 2:00 PM - 2:30 PM - Intelligent Manufacturing What if manufacturing systems and materials could sense, adapt, and respond? We often think of manufacturing as shaping raw materials into parts, but this talk explores a different vision: using intelligent systems to embed functionality directly into materials. Examples include self-monitoring additive structures, robotic ecosystems, and biomedical materials—moving manufacturing toward systems that perceive, decide,…
item.author: Community

item.title: Bird & Brine Delayed Opening April 10, 2026
item.description: Bird N’ Brine, located in the Student Union, will have a delayed opening on Friday, April 10, 2026, due to scheduled equipment maintenance. Please stay tuned for updates regarding our opening time that day by following Rensselaer Dining on social media @rensselaerdining
item.author: Community

item.title: Department of Chemistry Seminar Series
item.description: Date: Wednesday, April 8, 2026 4:00 PM - 5:00 PM - Glen R. Loppnow, University of Alberta and Cabezon Games, will be speaking on “The Future of Chemistry Education: Should We Be Teaching Answers or Questions?”
item.author: Community

item.title: Designing Sustainability: From Entrepreneurialism to Environmental Justice
item.description: Wednesday, April 8 | 4-6pm | Student Union Theater (1st floor) Come to this special panel event featuring guest speakers Alexis Goldsmith (Beyond Plastics), and RPI alumni Gavin McIntyre (Ecovative CEO), and Bobbi Wilding (Clean + Healthy Executive Director). Learn about their innovative solutions to sustainability and have a Q&A afterwards. Stay for a fun activity and food!
item.author: Community

item.title: AT:TENSION | Neurodiverse Sensing, Intelligences, and Dialogue in the Face of Uncertainty: A Multimedia 360º Symposium
item.description: In CRAIVE-Lab’s 360º environment (and online), the International Symposium on Assistive Technologies for Music and Art brings attention to our attention through various modes of dialogue. Four transdisciplinary artists, musicians, and thinkers open up neurodiverse and queer ways of knowing ourselves–as processes, nested within processes. The event opens and closes with 30-minute periods reserved for rest, surrounded by ambient sound and generative moving image. Join us on 4/8 from 2-6pm.
item.author: Community

item.title: MANE Spring Seminar Series Continues with Class of 1959 Distinguished Lecture Series given by Brian Argrow
item.description: Date: Wednesday, April 8, 2026 10:30 AM - 11:30 AM - Class of 1959 Distinguished Lecture Series - “Supercells to Supersonics: Aerospace Engineering for Atmospheric Research”, Dr. Brian Argrow, Distinguished Professor, Glenn Murphy Endowed Chair, Ann and H.J. Smead Aerospace Engineering Sciences, UC Boulder.
item.author: Community

item.title: Graduate Certificates Review with RPI at Work on April 8
item.description: Date: Wednesday, April 8, 2026 12:00 PM - 12:30 PM - Join RPI at Work Dean Aric Krause, Ph.D., in a 30-minute information session highlighting the whole range of RPI’s online, project-based graduate certificate programs. These certificates are three courses in length, developing targeted ability groups, and can be completed in as little as seven months. Please register if you plan to attend.
item.author: Community

item.title: CEE Seminar Series - Dr. Andrew Whittaker SUNY Distinguished Professor,University of Buffalo
item.description: Date: Wednesday, April 8, 2026 1:00 PM - 2:00 PM - “Risk-informed, performance-based design of seismic isolation systems for nuclear energy facilities”. Risk-informed pathways are being developed for the design and licensing of next generation nuclear facilities. This presentation will address one such pathway to implement seismic base isolation, which involves the development of a) isolation-system-specific seismic displacement demand curves from seismic hazard curves, and b) fragility…
item.author: Community

item.title: Webinar Friday April 10: RPI Digital Asset Management (DAM) System
item.description: Join Jim Evans, Director of MultiMedia Services, on Friday April 10 from 11am to 12pm, for a live webinar introducing Rensselaer’s Digital Asset Management System. Discover how to access campus‑wide photography, multimedia files, and institutional assets—all in one place. Register now: https://rensselaer.webex.com/webappng/sites/rensselaer/webinar/webinarSeries/register/783d951c6ab8482e9198a7bd6075a700
item.author: Community

item.title: Rensselaer Newman Theology Roundtable with Guest Speaker Fr. Mulcahy, OP
item.description: Join us Wednesday, April 8, 2026 at 7:30 p.m. to examine the development of 20th and 21st century Catholic teaching about migration, and to identify questions about the mass movements of peoples that often go unasked. How, if at all, does the large scale of contemporary migration change and challenge our hearing of the Gospel? All are welcome.
item.author: Community

item.title: Rensselaer Faculty and Staff Quick Learn Webinar: Tips for Improving Online Breakout Room Sessions
item.description:
Date: Tuesday, April 7, 2026 1:30 PM - 2:00 PM - In this webinar we will review tips and tricks to creating more productive breakout sessions. In this session, we will not be learning “how to” use the breakout room feature in Webex but we will be strategizing on how to improve the engagement in the sessions by offering roles, mixing things up, and creating connections. To register for this workshop and see a list of other upcoming workshops, please use the website listed in the See section.

For information about sharing your news or events, or to change your subscription settings, visit morningmail.rpi.edu.
item.author: Faculty and Staff
